title: Manage TLS Certificates in a Cluster — Issue a certificate
text:
{
"signing"
:
{
"default"
:
{
"usages"
:
[
"digital signature"
,
"key encipherment"
,
"server auth"
],
"expiry"
:
"876000h"
,
"ca_constraint"
:
{
"is_ca"
:
false
}
}
}
}
Use a
server-signing-config.json
signing configuration and the certificate authority key file
and certificate to sign the certificate request:
kubectl get csr my-svc.my-namespace -o
jsonpath
=
'{.spec.request}'
|
\
base64 --decode
|
\
cfssl sign -ca ca.pem -ca-key ca-key.pem -config server-signing-config.json -
|
\
cfssljson -bare ca-signed-server
You should see the output similar to:
2022/02/01 11:52:26 [INFO] signed certificate with serial number 576048928624926584381415936700914530534472870337
This produces a signed serving certificate file,
ca-signed-server.pem
.
